steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence immediately following the underlined portion reads: 'This incident leads me to the reflection that…' That signal word 'incident' tells us the underlined sentence must function as a concrete example that supports a broader principle about imagination and reality.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Yung achieved success as a writer by composing about imaginary voyages': focuses on writing success, which is not the point the author draws from the incident. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Yung's real experience of New York shaped his earlier imagination': inverts the relationship — imagination came first, then reality. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Yung's voyage illustrates a love of adventure': adventure is incidental; the passage's reflection is about imagination foreshadowing reality, not adventure. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the imagined voyage foreshadowed what Yung later actually experienced': directly supports the 'incident' that prompts the author's reflection — imagination preceding and anticipating reality. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
